sections.title: Getting Around Las Vegas
sections.summary: There is no subway in Las Vegas, but you rarely need a car. The Strip is walkable end to end, and the Monorail, Deuce bus, and rideshare cover everything else. Save a rental car for day trips to Hoover Dam or Red Rock Canyon.
bullets: Walking: the Strip is one continuous walkable corridor — most nights you won't need anything else.
Las Vegas Monorail: runs the east side of the Strip ($5/ride, $15 day pass).
The Deuce bus: 24-hour double-decker along the Strip and to Fremont Street ($6 day pass).
Uber & Lyft: reliable citywide and the best late-night option for off-Strip trips.
